title: geranium lamberti subsp. siamense
intro: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense, commonly known as Thai geranium or Thai plume poppy, is a plant species within the Geraniaceae family, native to Thailand. This plant is valued for its medicinal and therapeutic properties, offering health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating menstrual cramps. The therapeutic actions of Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense are attributed to its ability to stimulate the nervous system, acting as a nervine and sedative, while its bioactive constituents, including geraniol, linalool, and borneol, contribute to its medicinal effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, further supporting its use in traditional medicine.
benefits: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense has many benefits, such as promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, which can improve overall mental well-being and enhance the quality of life. This plant is also known to exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and potentially reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease. Additionally, it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in soothing skin irritations and reducing pain and swelling, making it a valuable asset in natural skincare and pain management. By incorporating Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense into one's daily routine, individuals can experience a range of benefits that can improve their overall health and wellbeing, leading to a happier and healthier life.
actions: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in people with arthritis, making daily activities like walking or gardening more manageable. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties also aid in wound healing, promoting faster recovery times for individuals with cuts or burns, allowing them to focus on work or family responsibilities without interruption. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant and antiviral properties help protect against diseases like cancer and HIV, giving individuals peace of mind and confidence in their health. By combating stress and anxiety with its adaptogenic properties, Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense supports mental well-being, allowing people to better cope with everyday challenges and live more balanced lives.
intro_study: According to "Bioresource technology", geranium lamberti subsp. siamense benefits from the use of paddy straw mulch, resulting in a 27% increase in essential oil yield.
constituents: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These compounds, including geraniin, geraniol, and linalool, have been found to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The plant's constituents also exhibit anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, thereby potentially reducing the risk of cancer and improving overall health. By utilizing the medicinal properties of Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense, individuals can experience improved physical and mental well-being, enhanced quality of life, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
parts: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of this plant are known to contain iridoid glycosides, including geraniol and geranic acid, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers of this plant are rich in essential oils, including geraniol and linalool, which have been used to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
preparations: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. The tea made from the plant is used to treat various health issues, including anxiety and insomnia, while its tincture is applied topically to relieve muscle and joint pain. The infusion, on the other hand, is consumed to aid in digestion and to reduce inflammation. Additionally, the plant's extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
side_effects: Geranium lamberti subsp. siamense can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's essential oil or extracts may lead to headaches, dizziness, or even liver damage due to the presence of geraniol and other compounds. Prolonged use or misuse of the plant's extracts for medicinal purposes can also cause interactions with medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions. Furthermore, using the plant's oil or extracts without proper dilution can cause skin burns or rashes, especially when applied directly to the skin.
